threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Tube instead of just 'the Overground' when you’re anywhere near Dalston. Give it a few more months.
Last night on the 59, a bloke serenaded us with 'Wonderwall' while a raccoon rummaged through someone's shopping. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard kids speaking three languages at once in the playground while the kebab shop next door blasted techno. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you can find a bar with swings instead of chairs. It's a bizarre mix of fun and nostalgia that you won't see anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they can name three taquerias before the first burrito's wrapped. It's a badge of honor, not just a meal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that was totally silent—no engine roar, just an eerie calm. Felt like a ghost bus rolling through the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think our plazas are just for selfies; locals use them for morning coffee, kids' soccer, and sunset chats. It's a whole vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're recent by the way they still constantly look at Google Maps for directions, even when they're two blocks away from home.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when someone mentions the 149 bus, not realizing it’s just a suggestion, not an actual schedule.
Heard a couple on the 59 arguing about which takeaway was best, then a lad chimed in about the missing chips from his last order. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw my neighbor's cat on a leash at the market, chatting with the vegan baker about oat milk—classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you ride a U-Bahn packed with people while a guy juggles on the overhead bars during a BVG strike. It's chaos, but it’s our chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local would eye-roll at the tourists snapping pics of the Dolores Park squirrels instead of the view. It's a whole ecosystem in there.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 near Valencia, and a guy started a live rap battle with the driver. Only in SF can you get a free show while stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí. They miss the small bakeries tucked away that serve the best xuxos, fresh out of the oven.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their overenthusiastic greetings to the grumpy buskers on Carrer de Verdi, thinking they’re still in a tourist bubble.
